title: mimulus tenellus var. japonicus
intro: Mimulus tenellus var. japonicus, also known as Japanese monkey flower, is a plant species belonging to the family Phrymaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to relieve anxiety, stress, and digestive issues, as well as its antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing. The herbal preparations of Mimulus tenellus var. japonicus typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for various medicinal applications.
